regime_read: ES & NQ moving together — broad agreement, more DURABLE directional signal. Trust the direction more.
narrative:
Macro backdrop —
[2026-06-30] US economic state (FRED):
  Growth: real GDP 2.1% (q/q ann)
  Inflation: CPI 3.9% YoY | core CPI 2.58% | core PCE 3.14% (Fed target 2%)
  Labor: unemployment 4.3%
  Rates: fed funds 3.63% | 10Y 4.38% | 10Y-2Y 0.28% (NORMAL)
  Consumer: UMich sentiment 44.8 | VIX 18.41

---
PRE-US-OPEN BRIEFING — ES/NQ — as of 2026-06-30 12:15 UTC

1) RECENT MOVE-BY-MOVE READ
Both indices are quietly higher and pressed near the top of their recent ranges. ES last 7505 sits right at its prior-day high (7505) and just under its prior-week high (7549.5); NQ last 30101.5 has nudged above its prior-day high (30069.5) but remains well below last week's high (30701.25). Daily returns are small and constructive (ES +0.06%, NQ +0.16%) while weekly returns are firmer (ES +0.91%, NQ +1.47%) — the NQ outperformance on the week tells you tech is carrying slightly more of the load, but only modestly. RSI mid-50s on both (ES 56.3, NQ 57.2) = trending-up, not overbought, room to run. Both trade above session VWAP marginally (ES 7507.57, NQ 30125.35 are reference points price is hugging) — price is essentially balanced around fair value into the print. Likely catalysts: the tape is sitting on its hands ahead of the 8:30 ET PCE inflation read (today) and the looming NFP (3 days out). News flow is dominated by geopolitics — fresh U.S./Iran talks in Qatar following weekend clashes — which is a two-way headline risk (escalation = risk-off oil spike; de-escalation = risk-on). The 'S&P 500 warning signal' headline is noise-grade clickbait, not an actionable catalyst. Net: orderly grind, no clean directional catalyst yet, event-driven coil.

2) VOLUME-PROFILE READ
ES: 5-day POC 7425.31, value-area 7390.55–7498.70, HVNs clustered 7417–7440. Price at 7505 is trading ABOVE the value-area high — acceptance above VAH is constructive (buyers have moved price out of balance to the upside). The line in the sand is value-area high ~7498.70; losing it pulls price back into the 7417–7441 accumulation shelf, with POC 7425 the magnet on any flush. NQ: 5-day POC 29666.79, value-area 29336.31–30048.10, HVNs at 29565–29692 plus a higher node at 30022.68. Price at 30101.5 is just above VAH (30048) and above the 30022 HVN — again acceptance above value, mildly bullish. The structural line in the sand for NQ is the POC at 29666.79; below the value-area low (29336) the breadth thesis weakens materially. CAVEAT: this is volume-AT-PRICE (where trade happened), NOT order-flow delta — I cannot tell you who was aggressing. Treat HVNs as gravity, not as proof of buyers.

3) NQ/ES LEAD-LAG REGIME
Classification: BREADTH. Contemporaneous correlation is high (0.942) and the lead-lag table is flat/noisy at every non-zero lag (best off-lag corr only 0.052, leader = NONE). No divergence. The return spread is essentially nil (20d spread ~0.00009), with only a slight 1d/5d NQ tilt. This is the 'good' regime for trusting direction: ES and NQ are moving together, so a directional move is more DURABLE and less likely to be a single-name/concentration head-fake. Implication: you can size a touch more confidently on direction THAN in a rotation/divergence regime — but note the regime accuracy on my own scorecard is poor (0.316), so I treat BREADTH as a trust-multiplier, not an edge. Reminder: this is ES/NQ research only — no execution path here (CME futures; OANDA is forex-only).

4) EVENT-CALENDAR AWARENESS
TODAY 8:30 ET — PCE, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge. This is the dominant near-term variable and the reason the tape is coiled. REDUCE SIZE into the window; do not carry full risk through the print. Tomorrow (07-01) 10:00 ET ISM Manufacturing PMI — secondary, trim into it. 07-03 8:30 ET Nonfarm Payrolls — high-impact, widen stops / size down; holiday-thinned liquidity around it amplifies whipsaw. CPI is ~12 days out, not a factor today. NOTE: PCE/NFP/ISM dates here are CADENCE ESTIMATES, not verified releases — confirm against the official calendar before sizing.

5) SYNTHESIS — META-REGIME + NARROWED BIAS
Meta-regime: low-volatility bullish drift in a BREADTH regime, with price accepting ABOVE the 5-day value area on both ES and NQ, RSI trending but not stretched, all above the 200-day (ES +7.8%, NQ +15.1%). That stack favors continuation. BUT three things cap my conviction: (a) we are pinned directly under prior-week highs (resistance overhead on both), (b) a high-impact PCE print is hours away — any directional commitment now is fighting event risk, and (c) live two-way geopolitical headline risk (Iran/Qatar). Clean structure but NOT a clean catalyst = I will not manufacture high confidence.

BIAS: LONG / long-the-acceptance bias, ~58%, invalidated below NQ 29666.79 (the 5-day POC). As long as NQ holds above value-area-high/POC and ES holds above its ~7498 VAH, the path of least resistance is a grind toward the prior-week highs (NQ 30701, ES 7549.5). A sustained loss of NQ's POC (29666) flips this to neutral and re-opens the value-area-low (29336). Practical execution note given the calendar: this is a POST-PCE bias — let the 8:30 ET print clear, then trade in the direction of acceptance rather than pre-positioning into the number. Confidence held at 58 because direction is clean (BREADTH, high corr) but catalyst timing is not, and my own regime-accuracy track record (0.316, n=9, still CALIBRATING) demands humility. This is intelligence, not a validated edge.
